title: Free Will Baptist Abolitionist Leaders and Pastors Endorse Warren Hamilton
author: James Silberman
orientation: Reports that the Free Will Baptist Abolitionist Society's founders and directors endorsed Warren Hamilton in the Oklahoma SD7 Senate runoff against Larry Boggs, framing the contest as a choice between regulating and abolishing abortion. The article reproduces three pastoral endorsement statements and the 2019 Oklahoma FWB resolution calling for the immediate abolition of abortion.
audiences: Oklahoma SD7 voter weighing the Hamilton vs. Boggs runoff
Free Will Baptist pastor or layperson considering abolitionist activism
Pro-life voter skeptical of the abolitionist critique of incrementalism
Abolitionist organizer studying movement-building inside denominations
Second Amendment voter evaluating Hamilton's broader platform

questions.question: Why does the Free Will Baptist Abolitionist Society argue that pro-life politicians have failed, and what alternative do they say Warren Hamilton represents?
questions.audience: Pro-life voter skeptical of the abolitionist critique of incrementalism
questions.answer: Pastor Sam Ketcher frames mainstream pro-life politics as a long-running negotiation with evil that has been ongoing since Roe v. Wade. He presents Hamilton as someone who will reject that posture and fight for the unborn without compromise rather than offering rhetorical support.
questions.quote: There can be no negotiating with evil and that is what pro-life politicians have been doing since 1973. If you would like to see what it really means to be pro-life, vote for abolitionist Warren Hamilton. Now more than ever, we need leaders like him who will do more than just give lip service. He will actually fight for the rights of the unborn without compromise.

questions.question: How did Pastor Kelly Greene first encounter Warren Hamilton, and why does that backstory matter to his endorsement?
questions.audience: Oklahoma SD7 voter weighing the Hamilton vs. Boggs runoff
questions.answer: Greene says he met Hamilton as a stranger in the Oklahoma State Capitol while lobbying senators about abortion, and that Hamilton's response to that conversation was to run for office himself. The story is meant to authenticate Hamilton as a movement-driven candidate rather than a career politician.
questions.quote: I met Warren Hamilton in the halls of the Oklahoma State Capitol while myself and other ministers of Jesus Christ were pleading with our senators to do something about the 5,000 babies a year that are aborted in Oklahoma. A stranger and I began to discuss the future of the fight against abortion and I was encouraged by his simple faith that abortion can and will end in our great state. This stranger was Warren Hamilton. Apparently, Warren decided to take matters into his own hands and determined to become the senator we needed that day.

questions.question: What non-abortion issue does Pastor Tim Gillespie cite to round out his endorsement of Hamilton?
questions.audience: Second Amendment voter evaluating Hamilton's broader platform
questions.answer: Gillespie, identified as the founder of OK2A, points to Hamilton's A rating from OK2A and pledges to trust him on Second Amendment protection alongside abolition. The endorsement signals that Hamilton's coalition extends beyond single-issue abolitionists to gun-rights conservatives.
questions.quote: As a father, pastor and activist for liberty, I fully endorse Warren Hamilton for Senate District 7. He is a fellow abolitionist and I trust him to protect our right to keep and bear arms, having received an A candidate rating from OK2A.

questions.question: What theological premise does the 2019 Oklahoma FWB resolution use to justify abolition rather than regulation?
questions.audience: Free Will Baptist pastor or layperson considering abolitionist activism
questions.answer: The resolution grounds its case in divine sovereignty, asserting that God's law is supreme over human law and that all human life is a sacred gift, which makes any abortion an usurpation of God's authority. That premise leaves no room for the regulatory compromises common in pro-life legislation.
questions.quote: WHEREAS, God's Word clearly declares that all human life is a sacred gift from our sovereign God and that His Law is supreme over mankind and man's law, and therefore that all abortion is wrong and usurps God's sovereignty

questions.question: Why does the resolution argue that traditional pro-life laws actually facilitate abortion rather than stop it?
questions.audience: Pro-life voter skeptical of the abolitionist critique of incrementalism
questions.answer: The resolution claims that pro-life laws function as regulatory guidelines that tell abortion providers how to operate legally, which the signers say amounts to facilitating the killing of unborn children. This is the abolitionist case against incrementalism in distilled form.
questions.quote: WHEREAS, traditional pro-life laws do nothing to abolish abortion and, in fact, merely establish regulatory guidelines for how to obtain a legal abortion and, in so doing, simply facilitate the murder of innocent unborn children

questions.question: What strategy does the resolution propose for finally overturning Roe v. Wade?
questions.audience: Abolitionist organizer studying movement-building inside denominations
questions.answer: Rather than waiting for the Supreme Court, the resolution argues that a state must enact a law abolishing abortion outright, and it calls on Oklahoma's Legislature, Governor, and Attorney General to pass, sign, and enforce such a law at all gestational stages and for all reasons.
questions.quote: BE IT RESOLVED, that we, the Oklahoma Association of Free Will Baptists, call upon the Legislature, Governor, and Attorney General of Oklahoma to pass, sign, and enforce legislation abolishing the practice of human abortion at all gestational stages and for all reasons in Oklahoma.

questions.question: Why does the article frame the Hamilton vs. Boggs runoff as a referendum on abolition itself?
questions.audience: Oklahoma SD7 voter weighing the Hamilton vs. Boggs runoff
questions.answer: The article notes that Boggs walked out of the Senate chamber just before the vote on the Oklahoma abolition bill, while Hamilton has pledged to author The Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act. The contrast lets Free the States cast the runoff as a binary between regulation and abolition.
questions.quote: Tomorrow's runoff will determine whether Senate District 7 is represented by a man who will work to regulate abortion or a man who will fight to abolish it.

questions.question: How does the article clarify the standing of the Free Will Baptist Abolitionist Society within the broader denomination?
questions.audience: Free Will Baptist pastor or layperson considering abolitionist activism
questions.answer: An asterisked note explains that the Society is not officially sanctioned by any FWB association and that pastors' titles are used only for identification, so the endorsements speak for individuals rather than churches or associations. This protects affiliated congregations from being read as institutional endorsers.
questions.quote: The Free Will Baptist Abolitionist Society is not an officially sanctioned organization of the National Assoc. of FWB or any state or district association. It is comprised of Free Will Baptist ministers and laypeople who wish to work toward an abortion-free society where life is cherished and protected. The endorsements contained herein do not constitute endorsements by any church, district or state associations, or the national association.
